$comment: 'one owning module per table'. For every table listed here, the set of
non-test source files containing a WRITE statement (INSERT [OR ...] INTO /
UPDATE ... SET / DELETE FROM / REPLACE INTO) must EXACTLY equal the
checks each table exists in migrations/expected-schema.txt.
Migrations (*.sql + the runner) and test files are exempt: schema
evolution and fixtures are not runtime writers.
To extend coverage to another table, add an entry with its honest
current writer set — the test picks it up automatically.

notes: gateway/projects/sqlite-store.ts is the owning settings/lifecycle store
(seed, PATCH, archive/restore, activity stamps — incl.
touchActivityIncludingArchived, moved verbatim from open/composer.ts).
gateway/wiring/project-create.ts is the sanctioned
project-CREATION writer (refactor-plan §P4: creation converges on
sqlite-store.ts + project-create.ts; the second creation writer at
onboarding/wow-moment/actions/03-project-shells.ts was deleted by
K11b1 before this unit landed, so there is nothing to converge).
cores/free/agent-settings/src/backend.ts is a RETAINED direct writer
(rename/delete/archive/restore/merge/engagement-mode): it lives in the
cores band, where importing the gateway store would violate the
cores-use-sdk-only dependency rule. Folding it in needs a
dependency-inversion seam (inject a lifecycle-writer interface built
in the gateway wiring) — deliberate follow-up, out of P4 scope.
FOLLOW-UP: touchActivity vs touchActivityIncludingArchived differ only
by 'archived_at IS NULL'; converge deliberately once the archived-row
stamp semantics are decided (byte-identical-move rule kept them apart).
Single-writer. The telemetry factory's mint-on-miss attempt_id
transaction (onboarding/telemetry/event-emitter.ts) moved verbatim
into SqliteOnboardingStateStore.resolveOrMintAttemptId in P4.
resume-cron's SQL json_remove writer (the plan's second dialect) was
deleted by K11b1 before this unit landed — the store's JS
shallow-merge is now the only phase_state write dialect.
P9 (world-class refactor) — GBrain sync observability. Single-writer:
the `createGbrainSyncStateStore` sink UPSERTs the one per-scope health
row (status/latch/last-success/deferred-count). It is the concrete
`GbrainSyncStateSink` implementation injected into GBrainSyncHook; the
hook (gbrain-memory, platform band) owns no DB and only calls the port,
so the write stays in the composition band where DB access lives.
Pure side-observation — this unit adds visibility, not behavior; the
fail-soft sync path is byte-identical.
Executor-mode reminders (plan task 4; migration 0106 PART B). Durable
ritual run history — one row per fire ATTEMPT (skips included), retained
on its own window (NOT pruned on the subagent liveness prune). Single-
writer: `createRitualRunStore` owns every INSERT (skipped/running/failed)
and the terminal UPDATE (finished/failed/timed_out/crashed + ended_at +
truncated output_summary). The retention prune lands with plan task 5.
